findings.message: The newly added `requests` dependency is unpinned (no version specifier), violating the convention used by every other dependency in this manifest (all use `>=` minimum bounds, e.g. `fastapi>=0.115`, `tenacity>=9.0`). An unpinned dep accepts any version, including future majors with breaking changes, hurting build reproducibility and supply-chain risk posture.

The `requests` library is likely redundant in this project. The runtime already depends on `httpx>=0.28` (in the dev group, but commonly used as the FastAPI/async HTTP client), and `httpx` provides a `requests`-compatible synchronous API. Adding `requests` introduces a second HTTP client with overlapping responsibility, extra transitive deps (urllib3, charset-normalizer, certifi, idna), and no async support — a poor fit for a FastAPI/OTel-instrumented service where `httpx` is already instrumented by `opentelemetry-instrumentation-httpx`. Justification for the addition is not evident from the diff.
findings.suggestion: Use the existing `httpx` (move to runtime deps if needed) for outbound HTTP calls instead of adding `requests`; `httpx.Client()` offers a near drop-in sync API and integrates with the project's OTel stack.

findings.message: Maintenance/supply-chain note: `requests` is widely used and actively maintained, but it is in a long-term maintenance mode with no async support and historically slow release cadence. For a project already standardized on the modern async stack (FastAPI, OTel, SQLAlchemy 2.x, psycopg 3), prefer `httpx` for consistency and better observability integration.
findings.suggestion: Document the justification for `requests` if it must stay (e.g. a third-party SDK requires it), otherwise drop in favor of `httpx`.
